# Worked examples

Each example pairs a small input corpus with a `katalyst` command and shows the real output. They are generated by running the command and gated by tests, so the output here cannot drift from the shipped tool.

- [Check a valid item]({{< relref "check-valid-item.md" >}}): An item that satisfies its schema passes and reports OK.
- [Report a type error with a pointer]({{< relref "check-type-error.md" >}}): A field of the wrong type fails with a JSON-pointer diagnostic and exit 1.
- [Catch a title that does not match its H1]({{< relref "check-title-h1-mismatch.md" >}}): A markdown check fails when the frontmatter title and first H1 disagree.
- [Normalize frontmatter without touching the body]({{< relref "fix-normalize-frontmatter.md" >}}): fix canonicalizes frontmatter (sorted keys) and leaves the body verbatim.
- [Rewrite only the matched text]({{< relref "fix-text-forbids.md" >}}): A text_forbids fix template rewrites the violation and nothing else.
- [Cluster a raw directory by shape]({{< relref "inspect-source-shape.md" >}}): The raw-source document_shape inspector groups files into candidate collections.
- [Profile a collection's fields]({{< relref "inspect-collection-fields.md" >}}): The collection-layer object_fields inspector is a data dictionary over frontmatter.
